figures.caption: Fig. 34–1. A small cylinder of bismuth is weakly repelled by the sharp pole; a piece of aluminum is attracted.
Fig. 34–2. For any circular orbit the magnetic moment μ\Figmu is q/2mq/2m times the angular momentum J\FigJ.
Fig. 34–3. An object with angular momentum J\FigJ and a parallel magnetic moment μ\Figmu placed in a magnetic field B\FigB precesses with the angular velocity ωp\omega_p.
Fig. 34–4. The induced electric forces on the electrons in an atom.
Fig. 34–5. The possible magnetic energies of an atomic system with a spin of 3/23/2 in a magnetic filed B\FigB.
Fig. 34–6. The two possible energy states of an electron in a magnetic field B\FigB.
